tasks.title: Buy CT Pros domain + wire real website/email
tasks.notes: CT Pros has no website/domain yet. Buy a domain, then replace COMPANY_EMAIL, COMPANY_WEBSITE, MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS, GMAIL_OAUTH_FROM_ADDRESS placeholders.

prep.what: Purchase a domain for CT Pros LLC and update all placeholder email/website config so client-facing PDFs and emails stop using example.com addresses.
prep.understand: The app uses `office@ctpros.example` (RFC-reserved, non-deliverable) and empty `COMPANY_WEBSITE` in `.env` and `config/company.php`. These print on every proposal PDF and client email. Gmail OAuth also needs to be repointed once a real domain is in place.
approach: Check domain availability — try ctprosllc.com or ctprosconstruction.com on Namecheap; confirm with Rafa if he has a preference.
Purchase the domain and note the registrar credentials somewhere safe.
Update `.env`: set `COMPANY_EMAIL`, `COMPANY_WEBSITE`, `MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS`, `GMAIL_OAUTH_FROM_ADDRESS` to use the new domain.
Make a note in t62 that the Google OAuth client needs updating when the domain is verified (OAuth credentials live in Google Cloud Console).
resources: Google Cloud Console — OAuth2 credentials for Gmail
t62 notes in ct-pros-sys for full context

tasks.title: Derive wall PAINT area (perimeter × height) — the missing painting measurement
tasks.notes: A 2D plan has no wall height so painting gets ZERO auto-sqft today. Add wall/ceiling height input; wall paint SF/room = perimeter × height. Seeder currently DROPS geometry — preserve it.

prep.what: Add a `wall_height_ft` field to estimates and compute wall paint square footage per room from the engine's perimeter data, so painting estimates stop landing at zero.
prep.understand: The Takeoff Engine returns room geometry including `meta.perimeter` (in feet). `TakeoffEstimateSeeder.php` currently discards this geometry when building estimate lines. Wall paint SF = perimeter × wall_height_ft. Standard ceiling height is ~9 ft but Rafa's jobs may vary — make it a configurable estimate-level default with optional per-room override.
approach: Add a migration: `wall_height_ft FLOAT DEFAULT 9.0` nullable to the `estimates` table.
In `TakeoffEstimateSeeder.php`, preserve `room.geometry.rings` and `room.meta.perimeter` from the engine response instead of dropping them.
Compute `wall_sf = perimeter * (estimate.wall_height_ft ?? 9.0)` per room and emit a `Wall Paint Area` line via `LineItemFactory` (painting trade, own_team).
Add the height input to `Takeoff/Index.vue` or `Estimates/Edit.vue` — a simple number field defaulting to 9.0, scoped to the estimate.
resources: Takeoff Engine API response schema — `rooms[].meta.perimeter` and `rooms[].geometry.rings`
`Takeoff/Index.vue` or `Estimates/Edit.vue` — for the height UI input

tasks.title: Route takeoff measurements to trades at the seam
tasks.notes: Every measurement collapses into one anonymous kind=custom $0 line, dropping kind+geometry. Preserve+switch on kind: area(SF)->flooring, derived wall SF->painting, line(LF)->wall-linear/trim, count(EA)->doors/tile.

prep.what: Fix `TakeoffEstimateSeeder.php` to route each measurement kind to its correct trade line instead of collapsing everything into one anonymous $0 custom line.
prep.understand: The engine returns measurements with a `kind` field: `area` (floor SF), `line` (LF, e.g. trim), `count` (doors, fixtures). Currently the seeder ignores `kind` and creates one catch-all line. The `ProjectType::isCoreTrade()` helper (t36) already defines painting+flooring as own-team and trim/tile as subcontracted — use it here.
approach: Open `TakeoffEstimateSeeder.php` and add a `switch` on `measurement.kind`: `area` → flooring line (`fromLaborRate`, own_team), derived wall SF (from t45) → painting line, `line` → trim/wall-linear (`fromSubcontract`), `count` → door/fixture line.
Use `ProjectType::isCoreTrade($projectType)` to set `is_subcontracted` automatically per trade.
Ensure the seeder still builds valid estimate lines even when geometry/perimeter is missing (graceful fallback to $0 custom line).
Run the walk-through seeder against a test estimate and verify the Estimate Edit page shows trade-routed lines instead of a single anonymous one.
resources: Takeoff Engine API docs or existing SDK tests for measurement kind values
t45 output (wall SF per room) — needed as input to the painting route

tasks.title: Add PerLinearFt RateType (prerequisite for pricing trim/wall-linear)
tasks.notes: RateType has no per-linear-foot. Add PerLinearFt='per_linear_ft' (+ label), wire LineItemFactory::unitFromRateType->'lf', seed a per-LF trim LaborRate. Must land before takeoff pricing.

prep.what: Add a `per_linear_ft` case to the `RateType` enum so that trim and wall-linear measurements from the takeoff can be priced by the linear foot.
prep.understand: Without a `PerLinearFt` rate type, there's no labor rate row for trim or wall-linear, so t48 (pricing seeded lines by kind/trade) can't work. This is a small enum + seeder addition that unlocks the trim pricing pipeline.
approach: Add `PerLinearFt = 'per_linear_ft'` to `app/Enums/RateType.php` with a human label (e.g. 'Per Linear Foot').
Update `LineItemFactory::unitFromRateType()` to return `'lf'` for the new case.
Add a trim labor rate row in `LaborRateSeeder.php` (e.g. Trim/Baseboards, trade `subcontract`, `per_linear_ft`, ~$3.50/LF).
Run the seeder and verify the labor rates UI shows the new row without breaking existing rates.

tasks.title: Assemblies-per-room — paint/drywall/flooring quantities + wall heights
tasks.notes: PRD Phase 1. assemblies-lite folded into B1. Full version = room type → assemblies + formulas + heights (one classification → up to 8 quantities, CSI libraries). The last pillar between us and estimator-grade Excel.

prep.what: Expand the assembly engine so every room classification maps to a full set of estimator quantities — wall SF, floor SF, ceiling SF, LF counts — that feed the 5-tab Excel workbook.
prep.understand: The `assemblies-lite` scaffold in `engine/assemblies.py` was built during B1 (t6). A classification like `bedroom` should produce: floor SF (from area), wall SF (perimeter × height), ceiling SF (= floor SF), door count × rough-in, window LF. CSI format: division 09 (finishes) for paint/flooring. The `build_sheet_takeoff` orchestrator in `engine/excel.py` calls assemblies.
approach: Read `engine/assemblies.py` to understand the existing lite scaffold and what input it takes (room dict with area/perimeter/classification and wall_height_ft).
Define a `ROOM_ASSEMBLIES` mapping: `{classification: [{kind, formula, unit, csi_code}]}` — start with bedroom, bathroom, kitchen, living_room, corridor, office; add a wildcard fallback.
Implement `compute_assemblies(room, wall_height_ft=9.0) -> list[AssemblyQuantity]` applying the formulas from each room's entry.
Wire `compute_assemblies` into `build_sheet_takeoff` so the Excel tab gets real quantities per room instead of the lite stub.
resources: `docs/spike/` — any existing assembly research or wave notes
CSI MasterFormat Division 09 (Finishes) for category codes
PRD Phase 1 assemblies description in t18 notes

tasks.title: Label lane v2: M3 vision for outlined-text firms + callout-vs-room filtering
tasks.notes: Caught live on Harborview demo: rooms show 'unnamed' because PDFTron outlines ALL text. v2 = (a) productize the M3 tiles lane as opt-in 'read labels (AI)' action; (b) callout-vs-room filtering for noise like general notes, UP arrows, FEC callouts.

prep.what: Add an AI-powered label lane for PDFs where all text is outlined (zero extractable live text) and filter out non-room-name callouts from the results.
prep.understand: PDFTron-generated PDFs encode text as vector outlines — the live-text lane extracts zero labels. The fix is an opt-in M3 vision tiles lane using MiniMax (~4.3 min/sheet, metered). The spike script `run_labels_m3tiles.py` already proves it works. Also, on PDFs with live text (Holabird), the lane matches false positives: `UP` arrows, `FEC` callouts, multi-paragraph general notes — these need filtering.
approach: Productize the M3 tiles lane: wire `MINIMAX_AI_KEY` into the VPS `.env` and expose a `POST /sheets/{id}/labels?mode=ai` endpoint that triggers the tiles lane vs. the default live-text lane.
Port `run_labels_m3tiles.py` spike into `engine/labels.py` as `extract_labels_m3(page_image_tiles, model) -> {room_id: label}`.
Add callout filtering: reject matches where label text is ALL-CAPS ≤3 chars, multi-word sentence-like strings, or matches known fixture callout patterns (`FEC`, `AFF`, etc.).
Test against Harborview outlined-text PDF and Holabird live-text PDF — verify Harborview now shows room names, Holabird doesn't add noise.
resources: `run_labels_m3tiles.py` spike in `docs/spike/` — the proven M3 tiles approach
MiniMax API key (`MINIMAX_AI_KEY`) — needs to be in VPS `.env`
Harborview PDF (outlined-text demo that showed 0 labels)
Holabird sheet + 92/109 named rooms result — regression baseline
R13 polish docs for callout filter categories
